summary: Joel Spolsky shares a brief note about Fog Creek Software's summer internship recruiting season. He references a Boston Globe article that quoted him on how good employers build relationships with professors to find exceptional students. The post serves primarily as a call to action, asking readers to recommend capable students for Fog Creek's internship program. It reflects Joel's ongoing emphasis on hiring top talent as a core business strategy.
key_points: Good employers build relationships with professors to identify exceptional students early
Proactive recruiting through personal networks yields better intern candidates than passive job postings
Joel uses his blog's readership as a recruiting channel, leveraging his audience to source talent
money_quotes: The good employers have relationships with professors and they forward students who seem exceptional.
key_insight: The best hiring pipelines are built on relationships with people who can identify exceptional talent early, such as professors recommending their top students.
